title: Injured hiker rescued from White Mountains via helicopter
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A hiker was rescued by helicopter from Bondcliff Trail in Lincoln, New Hampshire after injuring his leg.
The NH Army National Guard conducted the extraction due to the remote location and difficulty of carrying the man out.
The injured hiker, a man from Plymouth, was airlifted to Dartmouth Hitchcock Medical Center for treatment.

title: State trooper hospitalized after crash with alleged wrong-way driver in Peabody
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A state trooper was injured in a wrong-way crash with a suspected drunk driver in Peabody early Sunday morning.
Lucas Gustavo Brajak DeAlmeida Benedetto, 41, faces charges including operating under the influence and negligent driving.
This is the second Route 1 wrong-way crash this month; new Massachusetts Senate amendment aims to prevent such incidents.

title: ‘A surreal experience’: Readers react to loud boom in Mass. caused by meteor
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A bolide meteor caused a loud sonic boom heard across eastern Massachusetts and nearby regions on Saturday afternoon.
Out of 285 respondents, 89% reported hearing or feeling the boom, describing experiences of shaking buildings and startled animals.
Residents initially speculated causes ranging from bombings to collapsed buildings before meteorologists identified the meteor as the source.

title: Mass. town warns of ‘neurological symptoms’ spotted in animals
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Athol, Massachusetts, reports wild animals showing neurological symptoms like stumbling and seizures.
Affected animals are mostly found in downtown Athol and Pinedale and Pequig neighborhoods.
Residents advised to keep pets vaccinated and report abnormal animal behavior to Athol police.

title: ‘It’s a special feeling’: Mickey Gasper never gave up on his Major League dream, and now he’s reaping the rewards
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Mickey Gasper, now batting .333, started his Red Sox season with the Triple-A Worcester team.
After six years in the Minors, Gasper debuted for the Red Sox in August 2024.
Red Sox interim manager Chad Tracy emphasizes Gasper's strong swing decisions and versatility.

title: Mass. family, Worcester woman identified as victims in deadly Virginia bus crash
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Greenfield family and a Worcester woman were identified as victims of a fatal Virginia bus crash on May 29.
The crash on Interstate 81 involved a charter bus colliding with vehicles, killing five and injuring 34 others.
Bus driver Jing S. Dong faces involuntary manslaughter charges; authorities are considering additional charges.

title: Connor Wong’s 3-RBI night helped power Red Sox win, and it couldn’t come at a better time for him individually
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Connor Wong contributed 3 RBIs, aiding the Red Sox to victory against Cleveland, enhancing his NFL standing.
Wong's potential home run was overturned to a double, but he managed to secure an RBI from the play.
Interim manager Chad Tracy designated Mickey Gasper as primary catcher, diminishing Wong's role in the lineup.

title: Sonny Gray continues to be great addition to Red Sox rotation, earns 6th win vs. Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray pitched six innings, striking out seven, in the Red Sox's 9-1 win over the Cleveland Guardians.
Gray allowed one run on four hits, lowering his ERA to 3.06 for the season with a May ERA of 2.00.
The 36-year-old right-hander has a mutual option for 2027, with a $10 million buyout clause in his contract.

title: Here’s the full lineup of athletes and celebrities involved in Drake Maye’s charity softball game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Drake Maye's charity softball game will be held at Polar Park in Worcester on Sunday.
Participants include Patriots' Julian Edelman, Bruins' Zdeno Chara, and comedians Matt Rife and Dave Portnoy.
The event supports the MayeDay foundation, assisting children and families facing hardship.

title: Potential A.J. Brown Patriots-Eagles trade reportedly has ‘framework in place’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: A.J. Brown could join the Patriots as trade talks with the Eagles reportedly have a framework in place.
The trade delay is due to salary cap considerations, with the Eagles preferring a post-June 1 deal.
Disagreement over a first-round pick remains; the Patriots are reportedly unwilling to part with it.
